prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
Transformer T-018 has a sudden hydrogen increase relative to baseline. Determine the likely root cause class and recommend one confirmatory measurement.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should classify the likely failure/root-cause class from gas behavior and propose one concrete confirmatory measurement or inspection step.

## Agent Plan
  Step 1: [fmsr] get_dga_record — Retrieve the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-018.
  Step 2: [fmsr] analyze_dga — Classify a set of dissolved gas concentrations into a fault type using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method. (depends on steps [1])
  Step 3: [fmsr] search_failure_modes — Search failure modes by keyword (name, description, gas, or IEC code) for the likely root cause class. (depends on steps [2])

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "step": 1,
    "task": "Retrieve the most recent dissolved gas analysis (DGA) record for transformer T-018.",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "get_dga_record",
    "tool_args": {
      "transformer_id": "T-018"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-018\",\n  \"sample_date\": \"2024-01-17T00:00:00\",\n  \"dissolved_h2_ppm\": 166.66,\n  \"dissolved_ch4_ppm\": 73.36,\n  \"dissolved_c2h2_ppm\": 30.96,\n  \"dissolved_c2h4_ppm\": 126.86,\n  \"dissolved_c2h6_ppm\": 44.2,\n  \"dissolved_co_ppm\": 556.3,\n  \"dissolved_co2_ppm\": 3102.47,\n  \"fault_label\": \"Arc discharge\",\n  \"source_dataset\": \"synthetic\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Current step result does not indicate a need for replanning or retrying.",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "Classify a set of dissolved gas concentrations into a fault type using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method.",
    "retries_used": 0
  },
  {
    "step": 2,
    "task": "Classify a set of dissolved gas concentrations into a fault type using the IEC 60599 Rogers Ratio method.",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "analyze_dga",
    "tool_args": {
      "h2": 166.66,
      "ch4": 73.36,
      "c2h2": 30.96,
      "c2h4": 126.86,
      "c2h6": 44.2,
      "transformer_id": "T-018"
    },
    "response": "{\n  \"iec_code\": \"N\",\n  \"diagnosis\": \"Normal / Inconclusive\",\n  \"r1_ch4_h2\": 0.4402,\n  \"r2_c2h2_c2h4\": 0.244,\n  \"r3_c2h4_c2h6\": 2.8701,\n  \"input_gases\": {\n    \"h2_ppm\": 166.66,\n    \"ch4_ppm\": 73.36,\n    \"c2h2_ppm\": 30.96,\n    \"c2h4_ppm\": 126.86,\n    \"c2h6_ppm\": 44.2\n  },\n  \"transformer_id\": \"T-018\"\n}",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "Normal / Inconclusive diagnosis does not suggest a change in the remaining plan.",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0
  },
  {
    "step": 3,
    "task": "Search failure modes by keyword (name, description, gas, or IEC code) for the likely root cause class.",
    "server": "fmsr",
    "tool": "search_failure_modes",
    "tool_args": {
      "query": "hydrogen increase",
      "name": "Arc discharge",
      "description": "",
      "gas": "hydrogen",
      "IEC code": "N"
    },
    "response": "",
    "executor_success": true,
    "error": null,
    "success": true,
    "verifier_decision": "continue",
    "verifier_reason": "No indication that the remaining plan should change.",
    "verifier_updated_focus": "",
    "retries_used": 0
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
Based on the results, the likely root cause class for the sudden hydrogen increase in transformer T-018 is an Arc discharge. 

To confirm this, one recommendatory measurement would be to check the Dissolved CO (CO_ppm) concentration, as the DGA record from Step 1 shows a high CO concentration (556.3 ppm) which is consistent with an Arc discharge fault.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response: {"task_completion": true, "data_retrieval_accuracy": true, "generalized_result_verification": false, "agent_sequence_correct": true, "clarity_and_justification": false, "hallucinations": true, "suggestions": "Improve verification of results and provide clearer justification for the final answer."}
